text.title: Action Item Carryover Tracker Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Action Item Carryover Tracker. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One log for the whole team / founder; not per-meeting / per-source. Distributed logs guarantee lost items.
rule.rationale: Single log surfaces patterns; distributed logs hide them.

rule.statement: Each row has: id / what / owner / source_meeting / created_at / due_at / carryover_count / status.
rule.rationale: Missing fields = unverifiable claims of 'on it' / 'done'.

rule.statement: Carryover_count ≥3 triggers triage: kill / re-scope / escalate / re-commit. Do not silently re-add.
rule.rationale: Items with ≥3 carryovers are 80% likely to never complete in current shape.

rule.statement: Weekly: emit the stale subset (carryover_count ≥3) to owner + founder.
rule.rationale: Without the weekly emission, stale items live forever in the log.

rule.statement: Closed/killed items archive (status flag), not delete. Patterns matter quarterly.
rule.rationale: Deletion erases the carryover history that diagnoses bad estimation / capacity.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-action-item-carryover-tracker.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
